---
title: "1.7 Application Program Interface and Libraries"
subtitle: "Using Objects and Methods"
format:
  clean-revealjs:
    slide-number: true
    css: unit1-shared.css
lang: zh-CN
---

## Course Directory

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)

## APIs and Libraries

### Reusing code written by other programmers

We have already been using `System.out.println()`. That method is part of the Java <span class="term">API</span>.

::: {.tight-list}
- a <span class="term">library</span> is a collection of classes written by other programmers
- an <span class="term">API</span> tells you how to use those classes
- a <span class="term">package</span> organizes related classes so they can be imported and reused
:::

The practical point is simple: <span class="mark">you do not write everything from scratch</span>.

## java.lang Package

### Built-in classes you already use

The package <span class="term">`java.lang`</span> is available by default in Java.

::: {.tight-list}
- `String` is in `java.lang`
- `System` is in `java.lang`
- `System.out` is an object that can print
- `println()` is one of the methods provided by `PrintStream`
:::

Documentation matters because it tells you the available methods and which types they accept.

## Turtle Library

### A concrete example of an API

The textbook uses a `Turtle` library so students can see objects doing visible work.

![Turtle attributes and behaviors](../../assets/1-using-objects-and-methods/1-7-application-program-interface-and-libraries/turtleOOD.png){fig-align="center" width="54%"}

The class defines both <span class="term">attributes</span> and <span class="term">behaviors</span>.

## Attributes and Methods

### How to tell them apart in documentation

::: {.tight-list}
- attributes are stored data such as `name`, `width`, or `height`
- methods are actions such as `forward()` and `turnLeft()`
- methods always appear with <span class="mark">parentheses</span>
- attributes do <span class="mark">not</span> use parentheses
:::

This is one of the first documentation-reading skills students need in Java.

## The Dot Operator

### Asking an object to do something

```java
yertle.forward();
yertle.turnRight();
System.out.println("Hi");
```

The <span class="term">dot operator</span> `.` connects the object or class with the method being used.

Read `yertle.forward()` as: ask the turtle object `yertle` to execute its `forward()` behavior.

## Classroom Tasks

### Practice worth keeping

Retained classroom work for this topic:

::: {.tight-list}
- vocabulary checks for API, library, package, class, and method
- turtle direction and method-identification checks
- mixed-up turtle code tracing
- <span class="term">1.7.3 Coding Challenge: Turtle Drawing</span>
:::

This topic is mainly about <span class="mark">reading and using existing code</span>.

## Classroom Check

### A complete answer should...

::: {.tight-list}
- distinguish <span class="term">library</span>, <span class="term">API</span>, and <span class="term">package</span>
- identify `java.lang` as a built-in package
- explain that classes define attributes and methods
- recognize methods by their parentheses
- explain `object.method()` using the dot operator
:::

## End

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)
